title: F-001 з»“жћ„еџєе…ѓжЁЎеќ— (tree/grid/badge/section + ASCII fallback)
description: Build the W3-B structural-primitive vocabulary in two layers per C-003. (A) Add parity-trivial primitives to the SHARED theme: a section-bar renderer (в–Њ accent + bold title; none: '# ') and a scope-badge renderer ([team]=drift / [project]=ai / [personal]=human; none: plain text) in packages/shared/src/theme.ts, and byte-mirror them into packages/cli/templates/hooks/lib/theme.cjs so theme-parity stays green. (B) Add the COMPLEX CLI-only primitives вЂ” tree (в”њв”Ђ/в””в”Ђ/в”‚ with ASCII fallback +-/`- /|) and grid (padEnd-aligned columns + в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ rule line, none: -----) вЂ” in a NEW module packages/cli/src/tui/structure.ts (NO cjs mirror). structure.ts reuses padEnd/displayWidth/isColorEnabled from colors.ts. All glyphs gate ASCII fallback on isColorEnabled().
action: Create packages/cli/src/tui/structure.ts exporting: `tree(items: {marker?: string; text: string}[], opts?): string` (в”њв”Ђ for non-last, в””в”Ђ for last, ASCII +-/`- ); `grid(rows: string[][], opts?): string` using padEnd(col, maxWidth) per column + optional rule line в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ (ASCII -----) painted via `paint.muted` from ./colors.js (the colors.ts `paint` object's `.muted` method вЂ” NOT theme.ts's `paint(token, text)` function; they collide by name in this repo); both gating glyphs on isColorEnabled() from ./colors.js. Add to packages/shared/src/theme.ts: `export function sectionBar(title: string, colorOn = isColorEnabled()): string` returning colorOn ? `${ANSI.bold}${PALETTE.accent}в–Њ ${title}${ANSI.reset}` : `# ${title}`; and `export function scopeBadge(scope: 'team'|'project'|'personal', colorOn = isColorEnabled()): string` mapping teamв†’drift '[team]', projectв†’ai '[project]', personalв†’human '[personal]' (none: plain '[scope]'). Mirror sectionBar + scopeBadge byte-identically into packages/cli/templates/hooks/lib/theme.cjs and add them to its module.exports. Extend theme-parity.test.ts to assert byte parity for sectionBar + scopeBadge across all branches.

criteria: packages/cli/src/tui/structure.ts exists and exports `tree` and `grid` (grep -E 'export function (tree|grid)' packages/cli/src/tui/structure.ts returns both)
packages/shared/src/theme.ts exports sectionBar and scopeBadge (grep -E 'export function (sectionBar|scopeBadge)' packages/shared/src/theme.ts returns both)
packages/cli/templates/hooks/lib/theme.cjs defines sectionBar + scopeBadge AND lists them in module.exports (grep 'sectionBar' and 'scopeBadge' both appear in module.exports block)
theme.ts 7-token PALETTE values are byte-unchanged (git diff packages/shared/src/theme.ts shows no change to any '[38;2;...' line)
tree/grid emit ASCII fallback under isColorEnabled()===false (unit test asserts +-/`- and ----- appear, no в”њв”Ђ or в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ glyphs)
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- theme-parity && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: The structural visual vocabulary exists: tree + grid as CLI-only primitives, section-bar + scope-badge as parity-mirrored shared primitives, all with ASCII fallback, and the byte-parity test still passes.

files.change: New CLI-only module: tree(items) renders в”њв”Ђ/в””в”Ђ/в”‚ (ASCII +-/`- /|); grid(rows) renders padEnd-aligned columns + optional в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ rule (ASCII -----) painted via `paint.muted` from ./colors.js (the colors.ts `paint` object's `.muted` method вЂ” NOT theme.ts's `paint(token, text)` function; they collide by name in this repo). ASCII fallback gated on isColorEnabled() from ./colors.js.
Add sectionBar(title) (в–Њ accent bold / '# ' none) and scopeBadge(scope) (teamв†’drift/projectв†’ai/personalв†’human, '[scope]' core). Do NOT touch PALETTE/ANSI/paint/symbol values.
Byte-mirror sectionBar + scopeBadge from theme.ts; add both to module.exports.
Add assertions that themeCjs.sectionBar / scopeBadge equal themeTs equivalents on color-on and color-off branches.

implementation: Read theme.ts to confirm ANSI.bold/reset + PALETTE.accent/drift/ai/human are the exact codes to reuse; read theme.cjs mirror formatting (\x1b escapes, no leading-escape literal) so the mirror is byte-identical at runtime.
Add sectionBar + scopeBadge to theme.ts (named exports, default colorOn = isColorEnabled()).
Mirror the two functions into theme.cjs using the .cjs escape style (\x1b...) and append them to module.exports.

Create tui/structure.ts: import { isColorEnabled, displayWidth, padEnd } from "./colors.js"; implement tree() (last-item detection в†’ в””в”Ђ, else в”њв”Ђ; ASCII branch) and grid() (compute per-column max via displayWidth, padEnd each cell, join with two-space gap; optional rule line of в”Ђ repeated, painted via `paint.muted` from ./colors.js вЂ” the colors.ts `paint` object's `.muted` method, NOT theme.ts's `paint(token, text)` function; they collide by name in this repo).
Extend theme-parity.test.ts with sectionBar/scopeBadge byte assertions (color on + off).
Run pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build (schema/dist must rebuild before cli consumes), then theme-parity test, then tsc --noEmit.

reference.pattern: ux-w2-5 shared theme + byte-mirror parity; colors.ts width helpers (displayWidth/padEnd)

rationale.chosen_approach: Two-layer split keeps the byte-parity surface minimal: only parity-trivial primitives (section-bar/badge) enter the shared+mirrored layer; complex tree/grid stay CLI-only. This satisfies C-003 while keeping theme.cjs cheap to keep in sync.
decision_factors: colors.ts already owns width helpers (reuse, don't duplicate)
rationale.tradeoffs: tree/grid are not available to the HUD .cjs hook вЂ” accepted: HUD renders with the parity-safe shared primitives (F-005).

risks: theme.cjs mirror drift (escape style mismatch) breaks theme-parity вЂ” mitigated by extending the parity test in this same task.
Forgetting to rebuild fabric-shared dist в†’ cli sees stale theme exports (known pitfall: shared schema change needs build).
